text.title: Terraform IaC Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: One state file covers dev + staging + prod; a dev apply can taint prod.
antipattern.reason: Started with one workspace; never split.
antipattern.note: Fix: terraform state mv per resource to per-env workspaces. Use TF Cloud workspaces or per-prefix S3 keys. Update CI to apply per workspace.

antipattern.description: ../modules/vpc inline; one team forks it; drift between consumers.
antipattern.note: Fix: Publish vpc to private registry with semver tags. Update callers to source = registry.acme/vpc version = 'x.y.z'.

antipattern.description: Engineer ran state rm + import to rename; cluster recreated; outage.
antipattern.reason: Refactor via state ops instead of moved blocks.
antipattern.note: Fix: Revert via state pull. Re-do rename with moved {} block. Plan + review + apply.

antipattern.description: Module has if var.env == 'prod' ... in 30 places; tests pass for dev but prod plan differs unexpectedly.
antipattern.note: Fix: Extract env-specific values into per-env tfvars / locals. Module becomes pure: behaviour driven by inputs.
